text: Quiet place to study with calm music, lots of seating, good coffee, and outlets near most tables.
I brought my laptop and worked for two hours without feeling rushed. The wifi was steady and it was not crowded in the afternoon.
Cute cozy date spot with warm lighting, good wine, and a romantic little room in the back.
The service was attentive and the pasta tasted fresh. It feels more like a special occasion place than a quick lunch.
Affordable bowls with big portions. It is filling, tasty, and worth it for students on a budget.
The line moves fast and the broth is delicious. A great quick lunch if you are in Mount Pleasant.
Open late after midnight with quick service. It gets loud, but the spicy chicken is fresh and satisfying.
Good late night food when everything else is closed. Staff were friendly even with a long queue.
Excellent matcha latte, soft cake, and delicate pastries. A sweet dessert stop for cafe hopping downtown.
Cute vibe and rich matcha flavour. Seating is limited, so it is better for drinks than studying.
Family-run dumpling place with traditional flavours. The noodles remind me of homemade food and the portions are generous.
Authentic, legit, and great for family sharing. Large tables make it easy for groups.
Campus cafe with plugs, wifi, laptop seating, and students studying quietly between classes.
Good coffee near UBC, but it can get crowded after lectures. Morning is peaceful for work.
Fast counter service and reasonable lunch combos. Useful when you need a quick filling meal downtown.
Not fancy, but the staff are friendly and the portions are worth the price.
Tiny hidden gem ramen counter tucked away off the main street. The broth is rich and the place feels underrated.
Small local favorite with legit noodles. Not crowded, but there are only a few seats.
Great for groups because they have big tables, reservations, and sharing plates for friends.
Service can be slow on weekends, but it is spacious and easy for a family dinner.
Fresh croissants, sweet pastries, and calm seating by the window. It is cozy but not a full laptop cafe.
Dessert is delicious and the coffee is good. The bakery feels peaceful on rainy mornings.
Cheap student bowls near campus with generous portions. It is quick, filling, and budget friendly.
Good value Korean food near UBC. The room is casual and works well for a fast lunch.
Fresh sushi downtown with friendly service and a calm counter. It works for a quick lunch or a casual date.
The fish tastes fresh and the line moves fast. Not cheap, but the lunch set is worth it.
Small ramen bar with rich broth and legit noodles. It feels like a local favorite and is not too crowded.
Affordable bowls, quick service, and warm seating make it useful for a rainy day lunch.
Cozy izakaya with cocktails, warm lighting, and sharing plates. Great date-night vibe.
The service is attentive and the skewers are delicious. Better for friends or a date than studying.
Quiet matcha cafe with outlets along the wall, good wifi, and peaceful seating in the afternoon.
Excellent matcha latte, cake, and pastries. A strong dessert stop that also works for laptop time.
Traditional dumplings in Chinatown with big portions and family-style sharing. The food tastes homemade.
Authentic Chinese comfort food, friendly staff, and large tables for groups.
Cheap noodle cafe with filling portions and fast service. Good for students and quick lunch.
Not fancy, but the soup is tasty and the price is reasonable.
Family-run clay pot kitchen with traditional flavours and generous portions. Great for sharing.
The restaurant is busy, but service is quick and the food feels authentic.
Convenient bento near UBC with fast service, student prices, and filling portions.
Good quick lunch between classes. Seating is casual and turnover is fast.
Hong Kong style cafe with milk tea, baked rice, and affordable portions. It is busy but reliable.
Good value Chinese cafe downtown with quick service and a casual group-friendly room.
Bubble tea, egg waffles, and sweet desserts in a calm room. Good for cafe hopping with friends.
The tea is fresh and the seating is spacious enough for a small group.
Neighborhood sushi counter in Kits with fresh rolls and reasonable prices.
Quiet at lunch, friendly staff, and good value for a simple Japanese meal.
Bao bar with quick service, crispy sides, and cheap combo meals near Olympic Village.
Small hidden gem for Chinese snacks. It is not crowded before dinner.
Udon stop with fast service and student-friendly prices. Good for a quick lunch in Marpole.
The broth is simple but tasty, and the portions are filling.
Hot pot place with reservations, big tables, and sharing plates for groups.
Good for group dinner downtown. It is loud but fun and the service is attentive.
Peaceful vegan cafe with outlets, wifi, and quiet seating for laptop work.
Fresh plant-based bowls and good coffee. The space feels calm and not crowded.
Dessert bar with matcha cake, sweet pastries, and a cute date-night atmosphere.
Cozy lighting, good tea, and rich dessert plates make it a fun cafe hopping stop.
